required_proofs.label: Public GitHub repository URL
required_proofs.evidence: A public GitHub repository URL was supplied.
required_proofs.evidence_boundary: URL presence proves the publication target, not source freshness or remote CI.

required_proofs.label: Remote GitHub Actions run
required_proofs.evidence: GitHub CLI token is missing workflow scope.
required_proofs.required_action: Run `gh auth refresh -h github.com -s workflow`, then rerun this script with `--wait`.
required_proofs.evidence_boundary: Remote CI is required for public release completion but does not prove live model behavior.

required_proofs.label: Approved live model eval
required_proofs.required_action: Run an approved live eval, generate the review bundle, and score it after human review.
required_proofs.evidence_boundary: Dry-run or fixture scorecards are reviewability proof only, not live model behavior proof.

required_proofs.label: Reviewed comparative live eval
required_proofs.required_action: Run an approved live eval, generate the review bundle, and score it after human review.
required_proofs.evidence_boundary: Dry-run or fixture scorecards are reviewability proof only, not live model behavior proof.

supporting_evidence.label: Live eval harness smoke
supporting_evidence.evidence_boundary: Harness smoke proves the approved-runner path only. It is not live model behavior proof.

supporting_evidence.label: Competitive eval harness smoke
supporting_evidence.evidence_boundary: Harness smoke proves the approved-runner path only. It is not live model behavior proof.

required_actions: Run `gh auth refresh -h github.com -s workflow`, then rerun this script with `--wait`.
Run an approved live eval, generate the review bundle, and score it after human review.
Run an approved live eval, generate the review bundle, and score it after human review.
evidence_boundary: This audit classifies external completion proofs. PASS means the audit ran and labeled the evidence. Goal completion remains pending until every required proof is PROVEN or PROVIDED. Harness smokes, dry-run scorecards, and local package checks are useful release evidence but are not live model behavior, remote CI success, market-data correctness, trading performance, or public superiority proof.
